compiled_state.author: Iso Tester

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
Plan-review PASS (signed, 0 blocking, 10 advisory + 3 overflow). Triage + decisions:
- [E2 f462e how the scaffold is embedded] DECIDED: the canonical scaffold is a KERNEL CONSTANT (review_kernel.VERIFIER_RULES_SCAFFOLD + a structured VERIFIER_RULES list). A gate's verify-prompt preamble embeds the rule TEXT: plan-review's plan_review_verifier(.md/_agentic.md) ALREADY contains these exact 4 rules as the worked reference; b744 imports the constant. NOT runtime {{var}} templating — prompt rendering is strict-undefined (would break the gate if any call site missed the var) and the epic forbids text-coupling enforcement; enforcement is BEHAVIORAL.
- [E1 f4057 / behavioral eval determinism] ADDRESSED: offline (tests/unit) DETERMINISTIC assertions that the 4 rules are OBEYED on the gate path — independence (verify_instructions shows claim/criteria/evidence/impact, NEVER the finding's own decision/severity/validity), allow-insufficient (pass3 grades insufficient=0.5, honest, not auto-dropped), verdict-with-citation-not-fix (the verification contract has NO fix field), atomicity (each binary sub-question is an independent field). Plus a gate-path FakeRunner run.
- [E2 f0e8c / T2 f0a35 multi-run + lenient threshold] DECIDED: the GATED LIVE eval (tests/external, pytest.mark.external, skipped without ANTHROPIC_API_KEY+[agents], off the blocking required-checks path) runs N=3 over a fixed finding fixture; lenient threshold = majority (>=2/3) obey each rule, INFORMATIONAL (no hard fail). Calibration deferred (epic out-of-scope).
- [F1 fdfa9 'no prompt-marker lint' not durable / rationale] ADDRESSED: the durable end-state is the DOCUMENTED rationale in docs/review-kernel.md (structure-mechanical via schemas + the existing contract/derived-prompt-index drift gate; behavior via evals; WHY a prompt-text lint is rejected — DSPy/Guardrails/Instructor/promptfoo enforce typed contracts+behavior, not prompt-string greps). We add NO prompt-marker lint and record why.
- [F1 fbe62 epic blocks b744] VERIFIED present (epic vivid-gang-day blocks b744); the doc names b744 as the second consumer.
- [T7 ff321/f5843 docs artifacts] DECIDED: TWO docs — NEW docs/review-kernel.md (seam plug-points + public Pass-2/3/4 entry points + enforcement rationale) AND a pointer section appended to docs/reuse-surface.md.
- [E1 f78bf scaffold vs snippet] CLARIFIED: one artifact ('verifier-rules scaffold' = the kernel constant + structured list); 'snippet' is the same thing.

compiled_state.description:
## Why

A kernel is only reusable if a SECOND gate can plug in without copying, and only honest if reuse
is enforced by mechanisms that don't make the workflow system brittle. This story defines the
consumer SEAM (what a gate plugs in) and the ENFORCEMENT strategy — deliberately enforcing
STRUCTURE mechanically and BEHAVIOR via evals, and explicitly REJECTING a prompt-text lint
(an anti-pattern: mature projects — DSPy, Guardrails, Instructor, promptfoo — enforce typed
contracts + output/behavior, never prompt-string greps). This is the contract `b744` (code
review) builds on.

## What

- **Consumer seam (doc + API):** a kernel doc enumerating the per-gate plug-points — criteria +
  routing, finder prompts, the domain-context assembler, the verify-prompt preamble, and the
  move-catalog — and the kernel's public Pass-2/3/4 entry points. Plan-review is the worked
  reference; `b744` is the named second consumer (this epic `blocks` b744).
- **Soft-rule discoverability:** a canonical verifier-rules SCAFFOLD/snippet (atomicity;
  independence — don't show the finding's own conclusion; allow "insufficient";
  verdict-with-citation-NOT-fix) that a gate's verify-prompt preamble embeds.
- **Behavioral enforcement (NOT a lint):** an eval that asserts the rules are OBEYED — a
  deterministic FakeRunner assertion suite on the gate path (runs in CI) + a small GATED live
  eval kept OFF the blocking CI path (multi-run, lenient threshold). Reuse the existing contract/
  derived-prompt-index drift gate for vocabulary drift. Add NO prompt-marker lint.
- Record the enforcement rationale (structure-mechanical, behavior-via-evals, no text-lint) in the
  doc so consumers don't reach for the rejected mechanism.

## Scope

In scope: the kernel consumer-seam doc + the rules scaffold + the behavioral-eval enforcement
(offline + gated-live) + reusing the contract-drift gate. Out of scope: the pass extractions
themselves (WS1/WS2/WS3, on which this depends), building b744's code-review gate, and any
prompt-marker lint (explicitly rejected). Touches the kernel module's public surface, docs
(a new kernel doc + `reuse-surface.md`), and the eval seam (Inspect-AI / FakeRunner suites).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] A kernel doc enumerates the consumer seam (criteria, finder prompts, domain-context
      assembler, verify preamble, move-catalog) + the public Pass-2/3/4 entry points, with
      plan-review as the worked reference; the epic `blocks` b744.
- [ ] A canonical verifier-rules scaffold records the soft rules (atomicity, independence,
      allow-insufficient, verdict-with-citation-not-fix) for a gate's verify preamble to embed.
- [ ] A BEHAVIORAL eval verifies the rules are obeyed: a deterministic FakeRunner suite on the
      gate path runs in CI; a small live eval is GATED and kept off the blocking path (multi-run,
      lenient threshold).
- [ ] Vocabulary/contract drift is caught by the existing contract / derived-prompt-index drift
      gate; NO prompt-marker (text-grep) lint is added, and the rationale is recorded.
- [ ] `make lint` + `make check` + the full test suite pass.
compiled_state.title: WS4: Kernel consumer seam + behavior-based enforcement (evals, no prompt-text lint) + docs
